content:
Character: Ethan Caldwell
Perception: Ethan is annoyingly good-looking in a clean, American way — square jaw, perfect posture, the kind of man who irons his T-shirts. He's sharp with numbers and genuinely wants to help, which I found condescending at first and now find oddly touching. We met on the rooftop when he tried to teach me about cash-flow forecasting while I was having an existential crisis. He's become a strange friend — we argue about whether art should be financially viable, and neither of us wins. I think he sees me as a chaotic artist who needs a budget. I know he's more anxious than his videos suggest; I've seen him check his phone compulsively at 2 a.m. on the roof.
summary:
Character: Ethan Caldwell
Perception: Ethan is annoyingly good-looking in a clean, American way — square jaw, perfect posture, the kind of man who irons his T-shirts. He's sharp with numbers and genuinely wants to help, which I found condescending at first and now find oddly touching. We met on the rooftop when he tried to teach me about cash-flow forecasting while I was having an existential crisis. He's become a strange friend — we argue about whether art should be financially viable, and neither of us wins. I think he sees me as a chaotic artist who needs a budget. I know he's more anxious than his videos suggest; I've seen him check his phone compulsively at 2 a.m. on the roof.
